description: Commit format, allowed/prohibited commands, pre-commit memory sync workflow

commit_format.example: feat | schema: add external_sources_policy.md extraction target

mandatory_staging_before_commit.rule: ALWAYS run git status --short first, then git add -A to stage ALL changed/new files — NEVER manually list files for staging
mandatory_staging_before_commit.rationale: Manual file lists miss newly created wiki pages (e.g., wiki/comparisons/agent-memory-techniques.md) because agent doesn't track all artifacts across the session

allowed_staging_commands.description: Stage ALL changed/new files — respects .gitignore, catches everything
Stage only wiki directory changes (acceptable if no project-level files)

prohibited_staging_patterns: Manual file paths: git add file1.md file2.md — misses new/untracked files
git add * — includes system junk, temp files, logs

prohibited_commands.reason: System junk, temp files, logs — bloated index
No staged commits allowed — must be explicit

steps.action: Run git status --short to check all modified/created files — NEVER rely on manual file lists
Update working_memory.json: set focus_node = current task name; filter completed tasks from next_steps_todo
Update hot.md Active Project with Phase status + what was done
Run git status --short immediately after commit to check for untracked/unstaged files

steps.condition: If git status shows uncommitted files
steps.action: git add -A && amend the last commit (or create additional commit if needed)

post_commit_verification.rationale: Ensures no artifacts are left behind after commit — catches missed wiki pages, new scripts, updated WM

steps.action: Update hot.md System State → Recent Changes with summary of wiki changes

regular_wiki_memory_sync.scope: applies to all wiki operations regardless of work_mode
regular_wiki_memory_sync.note: Works alongside Pre-commit Memory Sync Rule (dev-mode only) — both can trigger simultaneously

process_references.lint_reference_note: Previously referenced as AGENTS.md#git-conventions in process-lint.json — now use rules/git_conventions.json#rule_id
